Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Analyze the Data I need to calculate the total duration for each Case ID to determine which ones are slow. Case 2001 Start 2024-04-01 09:00 End 2024-04-01 10:30 Duration 1 hour 30 minutes 1.5 hours. Attributes Resource CSR_Jane Adjuster_Mike etc Region A Complexity Low. Case 2002 Start 2024-04-01 09:05 End 2024-04-02 11:00 Duration From Apr 1 09:05 to Apr 2 11:00 Thats 1 day 1 hour 55 mins approx 25.9 hours. Case 2003 Start 2024-04-01 09:10 End 2024-04-03 09:30 Duration From Apr 1 09:10 to Apr 3 09:30 Thats 2 days 20 mins approx 48 hours. Case 2004 Start 2024-04-01 09:20 End 2024-04-01 10:45 Duration 1 hour 25 minutes. Case 2005 Start 2024-04-01 09:25 End 2024-04-04 14:30 Duration From Apr 1 09:25 to Apr 4 14:30 Thats 3 days 5 hours approx 77 hours.

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Identify Performance Issues Fast Cases 2001 1.5 hrs 2004 1.25 hrs Both are Low Complexity. Slow Cases 2002 26 hrs Medium Complexity. 2003 48 hrs High Complexity. 2005 77 hrs High Complexity. Conclusion High and Medium complexity cases take significantly longer than Low complexity cases Specifically Case 2005 is the worst offender.

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Analyze Attributes Correlation Complexity Low 2001 2004 Fast 2 hours. Medium 2002 Slower 26 hours. High 2003 2005 Slowest 48 hours. Correlation Strong positive correlation between Complexity and Duration High complexity triggers Request Additional Documents multiple times.

Name: Inductive Reasoning - IND
Text: Region A 2001 Low Fast. 2003 High Slow 48 hrs. Region B 2002 Medium Slow 26 hrs. 2004 Low Fast. 2005 High Very Slow 77 hrs. Correlation Region itself doesnt seem to be the primary driver Region A has fast and slow Region B has fast medium and very slow However looking closely at Case 2005 Region B High it has more document requests 3 times compared to Case 2003 Region A High 2 times Region B might have a slightly higher burden in High complexity scenarios but Complexity is the stronger factor.

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Resource Adjuster_Lisa Region B Handles 2002 Medium 2004 Low 2005 High. 2004 Low was very fast. 2002 Medium had 1 doc request. 2005 High had 3 doc requests. Adjuster_Mike Region A Handles 2001 Low 2003 High. 2001 Low was fast. 2003 High had 2 doc requests. Observation The specific resource Adjuster doesnt seem to be the bottleneck in terms of individual task time but rather the volume of work document requests they generate for High Complexity cases Adjuster_Lisa Region B generated 3 document requests for Case 2005 whereas Adjuster_Mike Region A generated 2 for Case 2003.

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Synthesize Root Causes Primary Root Cause Complexity Level High complexity claims trigger iterative Request Additional Documents loops Each loop adds significant time waiting for customer response re-evaluation. Secondary Root Cause Iterative Process Region B Adjuster Lisa Case 2005 Region B had 3 requests vs Case 2003 Region A had 2 requests for similar High Complexity This suggests Region B or Adjuster Lisa might have stricter initial evaluation criteria or less efficient communication channels leading to more back-and-forth. Tertiary Root Cause Resource Availability Workflow Manager_Bill handled the approvals for the slow cases but the delay happens before approval.

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Develop Recommendations For Complexity Triage claims better Route Low complexity to automated straight-through processing. For Document Requests Improve initial data capture Train adjusters to ask for all necessary docs upfront to reduce loops. For Region B Adjuster Lisa Investigate if they are more conservative Compare their approval rates or average doc requests against Adjuster Mike. SLA Monitoring Set alerts for cases entering the Request Additional Documents loop more than once.

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Self-Correction during drafting Wait I need to be careful not to overstate the Region difference Case 2002 Region B Medium 26 hours Case 2003 Region A High 48 hours Case 2005 Region B High 77 hours. Region B High 2005 Region A High 2003. Region B Medium 2002 Region A Low 2001. Region B Low 2004 Region A Low 2001. So Region B seems to have a slight negative performance impact specifically on High Complexity claims compared to Region A I will mention this as a specific observation but keep Complexity as the dominant factor. Resource Analysis Adjuster_Lisa Region B responsible for 2002 2004 2005 Adjuster_Mike Region A for 2001 2003 Comparing High Complexity Mike 2003 2 doc requests Lisa 2005 3 doc requests Conclusion The resource Lisa might be less efficient at gathering requirements upfront for High Complexity cases compared to Mike.

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Final Review Does this answer the prompt Yes Identifies slow cases analyzes attributes proposes root causes and mitigations.
